title: lepidium montanum subsp. glabrum
intro: Lepidium montanum subsp. glabrum, also known as mountain pepper or glabrous pepperwort, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the mustard family (Brassicaceae) that is native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Lepidium montanum subsp. glabrum include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and support immune function. Lepidium montanum subsp. glabrum can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
